title: UCNFI Committee Member Record
description: Schema for a single committee member record in the UCNFI committee directory. Holds verified identifiers, enrichment-derived expertise tags, opportunity area mapping, role facets, member-facing self-report, and reconciliation. Pass 3 fields (positions, sensitivities) reserved but not required in v1.

member_id.description: Stable identifier, slug form. Convention: lowercase-hyphenated last name plus first initial (e.g., 'khosla-p', 'noble-s'). Do not change once assigned.

full.description: Full name as the member uses it publicly.

preferred.description: Preferred form of address if different from full name.

pronouns.description: Pronouns if known and shared publicly.

organization.description: Primary employing organization (e.g., 'UC San Diego', 'Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory').

campus_or_unit.description: Specific campus, school, division, or unit if relevant.

secondary_affiliations.description: Additional appointments, courtesy appointments, lab affiliations, center directorships, governance roles outside primary employment.

represents.description: Constituency or perspective this member is understood to bring to the committee. Free-text array (e.g., 'undergraduate students', 'shared governance', 'state government', 'national lab community'). Used where domain expertise tags don't capture the reason someone is on the committee.

contact.description: Contact information. Treated as sensitive; populate from authoritative source only.

assistant_contact.description: Name and email of executive assistant or scheduler if relevant.

enrichment.description: Enrichment-derived assessments. This is the team's view of the member based on public sources and prior committee documents. Member's own self-report lives in the self_report field below.

expertise_tags.description: Domain expertise tags from the controlled vocabulary. Cap of 3 unless the public record genuinely supports more. Each tag has its own confidence rating and supporting evidence.

tag.description: Tag from the controlled vocabulary. See expertise-taxonomy.json for current valid values.

confidence.description: How well the public record supports this tag. High = explicitly named. Medium = implied or adjacent. Low = inferred from background, flag for verification.

evidence.description: Brief note on why this tag was assigned. References specific sources where useful.

opportunity_areas.description: Mapping to UCNFI Opportunity Areas (OA-1 through OA-8). Cap of 3, with one designated primary.

rationale.description: One-sentence explanation of why this OA fits.

role_facets.description: Facets that aren't expertise but shape how the member contributes and how the RAG should present them.

ai_relationship.description: How this member relates to AI as a domain. Multi-select.

governance_orientation.description: Domains of governance this member is positioned to speak to.

synopsis.description: 3-5 sentence summary suitable for retrieval. Plain language, present tense. What this member does, what they bring to the committee, what perspective they hold. No marketing language.

sources.description: Source URLs and references supporting the enrichment. Each claim in the synopsis or tag list should trace to at least one source here.

note.description: What this source contributes that others don't.

pass_3_reserved.description: Reserved for Pass 3 enrichment (positions, sensitivities, recent public statements). Not populated in v1; structure will be defined when Pass 3 begins.

self_report.description: Member's own self-reported expertise and interests, captured via the inaugural-meeting survey. Distinct from enrichment. Disagreements between the two are signal, not error.

expertise_tags_claimed.description: Tags the member selected from the member-facing taxonomy. The member-facing taxonomy may be a refined version of the enrichment taxonomy; mapping is documented in taxonomy-version-history.md.

opportunity_areas_interest.description: OAs the member indicated interest in engaging with. May differ from enrichment-assigned OAs.

self_description.description: Free-text response to a survey prompt asking the member to describe what they bring to the committee in their own words.

categories_missing.description: Free-text response to a survey prompt asking what the offered categories failed to capture about the member's expertise or perspective.

leadership_willingness.description: Member's stated willingness to take on visible roles for the committee.

reconciliation.description: Derived view comparing enrichment to self-report. Populated after self-report is submitted. Discrepancies are tracked here; this is the working artifact for working group composition decisions.

tags_added_by_enrichment.description: Tags the public record supports but the member did not claim. Reconciler's judgment whether to keep or drop.

tags_claimed_not_supported.description: Tags the member claimed but the public record does not clearly support. Often legitimate (private work, recent transitions). Flag for discussion rather than reject.

final_tags.description: The reconciled tag set used for working group composition and downstream decisions. After reconciliation, this is the operational answer.

notes.description: Reconciler's notes on judgment calls, open questions, or items to revisit.

record_meta.description: Provenance and maintenance metadata for the record itself.

last_verified.description: Date the enrichment fields were last checked against current sources.

fields_updated_this_pass.description: Which top-level fields were checked or updated in the most recent verification pass. Audit trail without per-field timestamping.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the record conforms to. Increment when schema changes.

enrichment_pass.description: Highest enrichment pass completed for this record.

needs_attention.description: Open items flagged for follow-up. Cleared when resolved.
